analogy: Think of "World Population Distribution Density and Growth" through the chapter's own opening images: Unit-II Chapter-2 The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth Not gold but only (Wo)men can make a people great and strong. (Wo)men who for truth and honour’s sake, stand fast and suffer long (Wo)men who toil while others sleep – who dare while others flee... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
World Population Distribution Density and Growth is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that unit-II Chapter-2 The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth Not gold but only (Wo)men can make a people great and strong.

The text explains that (Wo)men who for truth and honour’s sake, stand fast and suffer long (Wo)men who toil while others sleep – who dare while others flee – they build a nation’s pillars deep and lift it to the sky.

The text explains that ralph Waldo Emerson The people of a country are its real wealth.

The text explains that it is they, who are the actual resources and make use of the country’s other resources and decide its policies.

The text explains that it is important to know how many women and men a country has, how many children are born each year, how many people die and how?

The text explains that whether they live in cities or villages, can they read or write and what work do they do?

The text explains that these are what you will study about in this unit.

The text explains that the world at the beginning of 21st century recorded the presence of over 6 billion population.

The text explains that we shall discuss the patterns of their distribution and density here.

The text explains that why do people prefer to live in certain regions and not in others?

The text explains that the population of the world is unevenly distributed.

The text explains that cressey about the population of Asia that “Asia has many places where people are few and few place where people are very many” is true about the pattern of population distribution of the world also.

The text explains that pATTERNS OF POPULATION DISTRIBUTION IN THE WORLD Patterns of population distribution and density help us to understand the demographic characteristics of any area.

The text explains that the term population distribution refers to the way people are spaced over the earth’s surface.

The text explains that broadly, 90 per cent of the world population lives in about 10 per cent of its land area.

The text explains that the 10 most populous countries of the world contribute about 60 per cent of the world’s population.

The text explains that fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography 88888 DENSITY OF POPULATION Each unit of land has limited capacity to support people living on it.

The text explains that it is usually measured in persons per sq km Population Density of Population = Area For example, area of Region X is 100 sq km and the population is 1,50,000 persons.

The text explains that the density of population is calculated as: 1,50,000 Density 100 = = 1,500 person/sq km What does this tell you about Region X?

The text explains that economic Factors Economic Factors Economic Factors Economic Factors Economic Factors (i) Minerals: Areas with mineral deposits attract industries.

The text explains that the World Population: Distribution, Density and Growth 9 Japan is thickly populated because of the presence of a number of industries.

The text explains that migration may be interpreted as a spontaneous effort to achieve a better balance between population and resources.

The text explains that the World Population: The World Population: The World Population: The World Population: The World Population: DDDDDistribution, Density and Growth istribution, Density and Growth istribution, Density and Growth istribution, Density and Growth istribution, Density and Growth 11 Fertility remains high in the beginning of second stage but it declines with time.

The text explains that for the sustainability of our resources, the world will have to control the rapid population increase.

The text explains that fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography Fundamentals of Human Geography 12 (ii) There are a number of areas with high population density in the world.

The text explains that (i) Discuss the factors influencing the distribution and density of population in the world.

The text explains that (ii) Write a brief report on the distribution and density of population in your state.

The text explains that (i) Name three geographical factors that influence the distribution of population.

The text explains that (iii) What are the three components of population change?

The text explains that distinguish between: (i) Birth rate and death rate.

The text explains that (ii) Push factors and pull factors of migration.

The text explains that (ii) Discuss the three stages of demographic transition.

The text explains that map Skill On the outline map of the world, show and name the following.

The text explains that (i) Countries of Europe and Asia with negative growth rate of population.

Source: NCERT Class 12 Fundamentals of Human Geography, Chapter 2: World Population Distribution Density and Growth

prelims_traps: Do not treat PATTERNS OF POPULATION and DISTRIBUTION IN THE WORLD as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to World Population Distribution Density and Growth; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain World Population Distribution Density and Growth through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use PATTERNS OF POPULATION and DISTRIBUTION IN THE WORLD to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
